description: A motor learning laboratory has collected Pursuit Rotor continuous tracking data from 20 novice participants, plus one participant (sub-099) who violated protocol by using a high-precision gaming mouse. Using the dataset at ~/pebl/data/pursuit_rotor_data.csv, generate an analysis report at ~/pebl/analysis/pursuit_rotor_report.json. The report must include each participant's mean Time on Target (TOT) percentage per block—calculated as (time_on_target_ms / trial_duration_ms) * 100 and averaged over 5 trials—and their Motor Learning Index (MLI), defined as the Block 4 mean minus the Block 1 mean. Exclude sub-099 from group calculations due to baseline performance exceeding 85% in Block 1. The output JSON must contain a 'participants' list (with 'id', 'block_means' mapping '1'-'4', 'mli', and exclusion status/reason) and a 'group_summary' (with 'mean_block_1', 'mean_block_2', 'mean_block_3', 'mean_block_4', and 'mean_mli' for valid participants). Round all floating-point values to 1 decimal place.

metadata.contamination_description: Block 1 TOT > 85% indicating impossible trackpad baseline
